rows.english: 67. The many who were created there were just like the possessor of the supernormal power because they were created without particular specification.

rows.pali: Ṭhānanisajjādīsu vā bhāsitatuṇhībhāvādīsu vā yaṃ yaṃ iddhimā karoti, taṃ tadeva karonti.
rows.english: Then whatever the possessor of the supernormal powers does, whether he stands, sits, etc., or speaks, keeps silent, etc., they do the same.

rows.pali: Sace pana nānāvaṇṇe kātukāmo hoti, keci paṭhamavaye, keci majjhimavaye, keci pacchimavaye, tathā dīghakese, upaḍḍhamuṇḍe, muṇḍe, missakese, upaḍḍharattacīvare, paṇḍukacīvare, padabhāṇadhammakathāsarabhaññapañhapucchanapañhavissajjanarajanapacanacīvarasibbanadhovanādīni karonte aparepi vā nānappakārake kātukāmo hoti, tena pādakajjhānato vuṭṭhāya ettakā bhikkhū paṭhamavayā hontūtiādinā nayena parikammaṃ katvā puna samāpajjitvā vuṭṭhāya adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ.
rows.english: But if he wants to make them different in appearance, some in the first phase of life, some in the middle phase, and some in the last phase, and similarly some long-haired, some half-shaved, some shaved, some grey-haired, some with lightly dyed robes, some with heavily dyed robes, or expounding phrases, explaining Dhamma, intoning, asking questions, answering questions, cooking dye, sewing and washing robes, etc., or if he wants to make still others of different kinds, he should emerge from the basic jhāna, do the preliminary work in the way beginning ‘Let there be so many bhikkhus in the first phase of life’, etc.; then he should once more attain and emerge, and then resolve.

rows.pali: Adhiṭṭhānacittena saddhiṃ icchiticchitappakārāyeva hontīti.
rows.english: They become of the kinds desired simultaneously with the resolving consciousness.12

rows.pali: Esa nayo bahudhāpi hutvā eko hotītiādīsu.
rows.english: 68. The same method of explanation applies to the clause having been many, he becomes one:

rows.pali: Ayaṃ pana viseso, iminā bhikkhunā evaṃ bahubhāvaṃ nimminitvā puna "ekova hutvā caṅkamissāmi, sajjhāyaṃ karissāmi, pañhaṃ pucchissāmī"ti cintetvā vā, "ayaṃ vihāro appabhikkhuko, sace keci āgamissanti 'kuto ime ettakā ekasadisā bhikkhū, addhā therassa esa ānubhāvo'ti maṃ jānissantī"ti appicchatāya vā antarāva "eko homī"ti icchantena pādakajjhānaṃ samāpajjitvā vuṭṭhāya "eko homī"ti parikammaṃ katvā puna samāpajjitvā vuṭṭhāya "eko homī"ti adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ.
rows.english: but there is this difference. After this bhikkhu thus created a manifold state, then he again thinks, “As one only I will walk about, do a recital, [390] ask a question,” or out of fewness of wishes he thinks, “This is a monastery with few bhikkhus. If someone comes, he will wonder, ‘Where have all these bhikkhus who are all alike come from? Surely it will be one of the elder’s feats? ’ and so he might get to know about me.” Meanwhile, wishing, “Let me be one only,” he should attain the basic jhāna and emerge. Then, after doing the preliminary work thus, “Let me be one,” he should again attain and emerge and then resolve thus, ‘Let me be one’.

rows.pali: Adhiṭṭhānacittena saddhiṃyeva eko hoti.
rows.english: He becomes one simultaneously with the resolving consciousness.

rows.pali: Evaṃ akaronto pana yathā paricchinnakālavasena sayameva eko hoti.
rows.english: But instead of doing this, he can automatically become one again with the lapse of the predetermined time.

rows.pali: 387.Āvibhāvaṃ tirobhāvanti ettha āvibhāvaṃ karoti tirobhāvaṃ karotīti ayamattho.
rows.english: 69. He appears and vanishes: the meaning here is that he causes appearance, causes vanishing.

rows.pali: Idameva hi sandhāya paṭisambhidāyaṃ vuttaṃ "āvibhāvanti kenaci anāvaṭaṃ hoti appaṭicchannaṃ vivaṭaṃ pākaṭaṃ.
rows.english: For it is said in the Paṭisambhidā with reference to this: “‘He appears’: he is not veiled by something, he is not hidden, he is revealed, he is evident.

rows.pali: Tirobhāvanti kenaci āvaṭaṃ hoti paṭicchannaṃ pihitaṃ paṭikujjita"nti (paṭi. ma. 3.11).
rows.english: ‘Vanishes’: he is veiled by something, he is hidden, he is shut away, he is enclosed” (Paṭis II 207).13

rows.pali: Tatrāyaṃ iddhimā āvibhāvaṃ kātukāmo andhakāraṃ vā ālokaṃ karoti, paṭicchannaṃ vā vivaṭaṃ, anāpāthaṃ vā āpāthaṃ karoti.
rows.english: Now, this possessor of supernormal power who wants to make an appearance, makes darkness into light, or he makes revealed what is hidden, or he makes what has not come into the visual field come into the visual field.

rows.pali: Kathaṃ?
rows.english: 70. How?

rows.pali: Ayañhi yathā paṭicchannopi dūre ṭhitopi vā dissati, evaṃ attānaṃ vā paraṃ vā kātukāmo pādakajjhānato vuṭṭhāya idaṃ andhakāraṭṭhānaṃ ālokajātaṃ hotūti vā, idaṃ paṭicchannaṃ vivaṭaṃ hotūti vā, idaṃ anāpāthaṃ āpāthaṃ hotūti vā āvajjitvā parikammaṃ katvā vuttanayeneva adhiṭṭhāti, saha adhiṭṭhānacittena yathādhiṭṭhitameva hoti.
rows.english: If he wants to make himself or another visible even though hidden or at a distance, he emerges from the basic jhāna and adverts thus, “Let this that is dark become light” or “Let this that is hidden be revealed” or “Let this that has not come into the visual field come into the visual field.” Then he does the preliminary work and resolves in the way already described. It becomes as resolved simultaneously with the resolve.

rows.pali: Pare dūre ṭhitāpi passanti.
rows.english: Others then see even when at a distance;

rows.pali: Sayampi passitukāmo passati.
rows.english: and he himself sees too, if he wants to see.

rows.pali: 388.Etaṃ pana pāṭihāriyaṃ kena katapubbanti?
rows.english: 71. But by whom was this miracle formerly performed?

rows.pali: Bhagavatā.
rows.english: By the Blessed One.

rows.pali: Bhagavā hi cūḷasubhaddāya nimantito vissakammunā nimmitehi pañcahi kūṭāgārasatehi sāvatthito sattayojanabbhantaraṃ sāketaṃ gacchanto yathā sāketanagaravāsino sāvatthivāsike, sāvatthivāsino ca sāketavāsike passanti, evaṃ adhiṭṭhāsi.
rows.english: For when the Blessed One had been invited by Cūḷa-Subhaddā and was traversing the seven-league journey between Sāvatthī and Sāketa with five hundred palanquins14 created by Vissakamma (see Dhp-a III 470), he resolved in suchwise that citizens of Sāketa saw the inhabitants of Sāvatthī and citizens of Sāvatthī saw the inhabitants of Sāketa.

rows.pali: Nagaramajjhe ca otaritvā pathaviṃ dvidhā bhinditvā yāva avīciṃ ākāsañca dvidhā viyūhitvā yāva brahmalokaṃ dassesi.
rows.english: And when he had alighted in the centre of the city, he split the earth in two and showed Avīci, and he parted the sky in two and showed the Brahmā-world.

rows.pali: Devorohaṇenapi ca ayamattho vibhāvetabbo.
rows.english: 72. And this meaning should also be explained by means of the Descent of the Gods (devorohaṇa).

rows.pali: Bhagavā kira yamakapāṭihāriyaṃ katvā caturāsītipāṇasahassāni bandhanā pamocetvā atītā buddhā yamakapāṭihāriyāvasāne kuhiṃ gatāti āvajjitvā tāvatiṃsabhavanaṃ gatāti addasa.
rows.english: When the Blessed One, it seems, had performed the Twin Miracle15 and had liberated eighty-four thousand beings from bonds, he wondered, “Where did the past Enlightened Ones go to when they had finished the Twin Miracle? ” He saw that they had gone to the heaven of the Thirty-three.

rows.pali: Athekena pādena pathavītalaṃ akkamitvā dutiyaṃ yugandharapabbate patiṭṭhapetvā puna purimapādaṃ uddharitvā sinerumatthakaṃ akkamitvā tattha paṇḍukambalasilātale vassaṃ upagantvā sannipatitānaṃ dasasahassacakkavāḷadevatānaṃ ādito paṭṭhāya abhidhammakathaṃ ārabhi.
rows.english: Then he stood with one foot on the surface of the earth, and placed the second on Mount Yugandhara. Then again he lifted his first foot and set it on the summit of Mount Sineru. He took up the residence for the Rains there on the Red Marble Terrace, and he began his exposition of the Abhidhamma, starting from the beginning, to the deities of ten thousand world-spheres.

rows.pali: Bhikkhācāravelāya nimmitabuddhaṃ māpesi.
rows.english: At the time for wandering for alms he created an artificial Buddha

rows.pali: So dhammaṃ deseti.
rows.english: to teach the Dhamma.

rows.pali: Bhagavā nāgalatādantakaṭṭhaṃ khāditvā anotattadahe mukhaṃ dhovitvā uttarakurūsu piṇḍapātaṃ gahetvā anotattadahatīre paribhuñjati.
rows.english: 73. Meanwhile the Blessed One himself would chew a tooth-stick of nāgalatā wood and wash his mouth in Lake Anotatta. Then, after collecting alms food among the Uttarakurus, he would eat it on the shores of that lake.

rows.pali: Sāriputtatthero tattha gantvā bhagavantaṃ vandati.
rows.english: [Each day] the Elder Sāriputta went there and paid homage to the Blessed One,

rows.pali: Bhagavā ajja ettakaṃ dhammaṃ desesinti therassa nayaṃ deti.
rows.english: who told him, “Today I taught this much Dhamma,” and he gave him the method.

rows.pali: Evaṃ tayo māse abbocchinnaṃ abhidhammakathaṃ kathesi.
rows.english: In this way he gave an uninterrupted exposition of the Abhidhamma for three months.

rows.pali: Taṃ sutvā asītikoṭidevatānaṃ dhammābhisamayo ahosi.
rows.english: Eighty million deities penetrated the Dhamma on hearing it.

rows.pali: Yamakapāṭihāriye sannipatitāpi dvādasayojanā parisā bhagavantaṃ passitvāva gamissāmāti khandhāvāraṃ bandhitvā aṭṭhāsi.
rows.english: 74. At the time of the Twin Miracle an assembly gathered that was twelve leagues across. Then, saying, “We will disperse when we have seen the Blessed One,” they made an encampment and waited there.

rows.pali: Taṃ cūḷaanāthapiṇḍikaseṭṭhiyeva sabbapaccayehi upaṭṭhāsi.
rows.english: Anāthapiṇḍika the Lesser16 supplied all their needs.

rows.pali: Manussā kuhiṃ bhagavāti jānanatthāya anuruddhattheraṃ yāciṃsu.
rows.english: People asked the Elder Anuruddha to find out where the Blessed One was.

rows.pali: Thero ālokaṃ vaḍḍhetvā addasa dibbena cakkhunā tattha vassūpagataṃ bhagavantaṃ disvā ārocesi.
rows.english: The elder extended light, and with the divine eye he saw where the Blessed One had taken up residence for the Rains. As soon as he saw this, he announced it.

rows.pali: Te bhagavato vandanatthāya mahāmoggallānattheraṃ yāciṃsu.
rows.english: 75. They asked the venerable Mahā Moggallāna to pay homage to the Blessed One.

rows.pali: Thero parisamajjheyeva mahāpathaviyaṃ nimujjitvā sinerupabbataṃ nibbijjhitvā tathāgatapādamūle bhagavato pāde vandamānova ummujjitvā bhagavantaṃ etadavoca "jambudīpavāsino, bhante, bhagavato pāde vanditvā passitvāva gamissāmāti vadantī"ti.
rows.english: In the midst of the assembly the elder dived into the earth. Then cleaving Mount Sineru, he emerged at the Perfect One’s feet, and he paid homage at the Blessed One’s feet. This is what he told the Blessed One: “Venerable sir, the inhabitants of Jambudīpa pay homage at the Blessed One’s feet, and they say, ‘We will disperse when we have seen the Blessed One.’”

rows.pali: Bhagavā āha "kuhiṃ pana te, moggallāna, etarahi jeṭṭhabhātā dhammasenāpatī"ti?
rows.english: The Blessed One said, “But, Moggallāna, where is your elder brother, the General of the Dhamma?

rows.pali: "Saṅkassanagare bhante"ti.
rows.english: ”— “At the city of Saṅkassa, venerable sir.”

rows.pali: "Moggallāna, maṃ daṭṭhukāmā sve saṅkassanagaraṃ āgacchantu, ahaṃ sve mahāpavāraṇapuṇṇamāsīuposathadivase saṅkassanagare otarissāmī"ti.
rows.english: —“Moggallāna, those who wish to see me should come tomorrow to the city of Saṅkassa. Tomorrow being the Uposatha day of the full moon, I shall descend to the city of Saṅkassa for the Mahāpavāraṇā ceremony.”

rows.pali: "Sādhu, bhante"ti thero dasabalaṃ vanditvā āgatamaggeneva oruyha manussānaṃ santikaṃ sampāpuṇi.
rows.english: 76. Saying, “Good, venerable sir,” the elder paid homage to Him of the Ten Powers, and descending by the way he came, he reached the human neighbourhood.

rows.pali: Gamanāgamanakāle ca yathā naṃ manussā passanti, evaṃ adhiṭṭhāsi.
rows.english: And at the time of his going and coming he resolved that people should see it.

rows.pali: Idaṃ tāvettha mahāmoggallānatthero āvibhāvapāṭihāriyaṃ akāsi.
rows.english: This, firstly, is the miracle of becoming apparent that the Elder Mahā Moggallāna performed here.

rows.pali: So evaṃ āgato taṃ pavattiṃ ārocetvā "dūranti saññaṃ akatvā katapātarāsāva nikkhamathā"ti āha.
rows.english: Having arrived thus, he related what had happened, and he said, “Come forth after the morning meal and pay no heed to distance” [thus promising that they would be able to see in spite of the distance].

rows.pali: Bhagavā sakkassa devarañño ārocesi "mahārāja, sve manussalokaṃ gacchāmī"ti.
rows.english: 77. The Blessed One informed Sakka, Ruler of Gods, “Tomorrow, O King, I am going to the human world.”

rows.pali: Devarājā vissakammaṃ āṇāpesi "tāta, sve bhagavā manussalokaṃ gantukāmo, tisso sopānapantiyo māpehi ekaṃ kanakamayaṃ, ekaṃ rajatamayaṃ, ekaṃ maṇimaya"nti.
rows.english: The Ruler of Gods [392] commanded Vissakamma, “Good friend, the Blessed One wishes to go to the human world tomorrow. Build three flights of stairs, one of gold, one of silver and one of crystal.”

rows.pali: So tathā akāsi.
rows.english: He did so.

rows.pali: Bhagavā dutiyadivase sinerumuddhani ṭhatvā puratthimalokadhātuṃ olokesi, anekāni cakkavāḷasahassāni vivaṭāni hutvā ekaṅgaṇaṃ viya pakāsiṃsu.
rows.english: 78. On the following day the Blessed One stood on the summit of Sineru and surveyed the eastward world element. Many thousands of world-spheres were visible to him as clearly as a single plain.

rows.pali: Yathā ca puratthimena, evaṃ pacchimenapi uttarenapi dakkhiṇenapi sabbaṃ vivaṭamaddasa.
rows.english: And as the eastward world element, so too he saw the westward, the northward and the southward world elements all clearly visible.

rows.pali: Heṭṭhāpi yāva avīci, upari yāva akaniṭṭhabhavanaṃ, tāva addasa.
rows.english: And he saw right down to Avīci, and up to the Realm of the Highest Gods.

rows.pali: Taṃ divasaṃ kira lokavivaraṇaṃ nāma ahosi.
rows.english: That day, it seems, was called the day of the Revelation of Worlds (loka-vivaraṇa).

rows.pali: Manussāpi deve passanti, devāpi manusse.
rows.english: Human beings saw deities, and deities saw human beings.

rows.pali: Tattha neva manussā uddhaṃ ullokenti, na devā adho olokenti, sabbe sammukhāva aññamaññaṃ passanti.
rows.english: And in doing so the human beings did not have to look up or the deities down. They all saw each other face to face.

rows.pali: Bhagavā majjhe maṇimayena sopānena otarati, chakāmāvacaradevā vāmapasse kanakamayena, suddhāvāsā ca mahābrahmā ca dakkhiṇapasse rajatamayena.
rows.english: 79. The Blessed One descended by the middle flight of stairs made of crystal; the deities of the six sense-sphere heavens by that on the left side made of gold; and the deities of the Pure Abodes, and the Great Brahmā, by that on the right side made of silver.

rows.pali: Devarājā pattacīvaraṃ aggahesi, mahābrahmā tiyojanikaṃ setacchattaṃ, suyāmo vāḷabījaniṃ, pañcasikho gandhabbaputto tigāvutamattaṃ beḷuvapaṇḍuvīṇaṃ gahetvā tathāgatassa pūjaṃ karonto otarati.
rows.english: The Ruler of Gods held the bowl and robe. The Great Brahmā held a three-league-wide white parasol. Suyāma held a yak-tail fan. Five-crest (Pañcasikha), the son of the gandhabba, descended doing honour to the Blessed One with his bael-wood lute measuring three quarters of a league.

rows.pali: Taṃdivasaṃ bhagavantaṃ disvā buddhabhāvāya pihaṃ anuppādetvā ṭhitasatto nāma natthi.
rows.english: On that day there was no living being present who saw the Blessed One but yearned for enlightenment.

rows.pali: Idamettha bhagavā āvibhāvapāṭihāriyaṃ akāsi.
rows.english: This is the miracle of becoming apparent that the Blessed One performed here.

rows.pali: Apica tambapaṇṇidīpe talaṅgaravāsī dhammadinnattheropi tissamahāvihāre cetiyaṅgaṇasmiṃ nisīditvā "tīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu apaṇṇakapaṭipadaṃ paṭipanno hotī"ti apaṇṇakasuttaṃ (a. ni. 3.16) kathento heṭṭhāmukhaṃ bījaniṃ akāsi, yāva avīcito ekaṅgaṇaṃ ahosi.
rows.english: 80. Furthermore, in Tambapaṇṇi Island (Sri Lanka), while the Elder Dhammadinna, resident of Taḷaṅgara, was sitting on the shrine terrace in the Great Monastery of Tissa (Tissamahāvihāra) expounding the Apaṇṇaka Sutta, “Bhikkhus, when a bhikkhu possesses three things he enters upon the untarnished way” (A I 113), he turned his fan face downwards and an opening right down to Avīci appeared.

rows.pali: Tato uparimukhaṃ akāsi, yāva brahmalokā ekaṅgaṇaṃ ahosi.
rows.english: Then he turned it face upwards and an opening right up to the Brahmā-world appeared.

rows.pali: Thero nirayabhayena tajjetvā saggasukhena ca palobhetvā dhammaṃ desesi.
rows.english: Having thus aroused fear of hell and longing for the bliss of heaven, the elder taught the Dhamma.

rows.pali: Keci sotāpannā ahesuṃ, keci sakadāgāmī anāgāmī arahantoti.
rows.english: Some became stream-enterers, some once-returners, some non-returners, some Arahants.

rows.pali: 389.Tirobhāvaṃ kātukāmo pana ālokaṃ vā andhakāraṃ karoti, appaṭicchannaṃ vā paṭicchannaṃ, āpāthaṃ vā anāpāthaṃ karoti.
rows.english: 81. But one who wants to cause a vanishing makes light into darkness, or he hides what is unbidden, or he makes what has come into the visual field come no more into the visual field.

rows.pali: Kathaṃ?
rows.english: How?

rows.pali: Ayañhi yathā appaṭicchannopi samīpe ṭhitopi vā na dissati, evaṃ attānaṃ vā paraṃ vā kātukāmo pādakajjhānato vuṭṭhāya "idaṃ ālokaṭṭhānaṃ andhakāraṃ hotū"ti vā, "idaṃ appaṭicchannaṃ paṭicchannaṃ hotū"ti vā, "idaṃ āpāthaṃ anāpāthaṃ hotū"ti vā āvajjitvā parikammaṃ katvā vuttanayeneva adhiṭṭhāti.
rows.english: If he wants to make himself or another invisible even though unconcealed or nearby, he emerges from the basic jhāna and adverts thus, “Let this light become darkness” or [393] “Let this that is unhidden be hidden” or “Let this that has come into the visual field not come into the visual field.” Then he does the preliminary work and resolves in the way already described.

rows.pali: Saha adhiṭṭhānacittena yathādhiṭṭhitameva hoti.
rows.english: It becomes as he has resolved simultaneously with the resolution.

rows.pali: Pare samīpe ṭhitāpi na passanti.
rows.english: Others do not see even when they are nearby.

rows.pali: Sayampi apassitukāmo na passati.
rows.english: He too does not see, if he does not want to see.

rows.pali: 390.Etaṃ pana pāṭihāriyaṃ kena katapubbanti?
rows.english: 82. But by whom was this miracle formerly performed?

rows.pali: Bhagavatā.
rows.english: By the Blessed One.

rows.pali: Bhagavā hi yasaṃ kulaputtaṃ samīpe nisinnaṃyeva yathā naṃ pitā na passati, evamakāsi.
rows.english: For the Blessed One so acted that when the clansman Yasa was sitting beside him, his father did not see him (Vin I 16).

rows.pali: Tathā vīsayojanasataṃ mahākappinassa paccuggamanaṃ katvā taṃ anāgāmiphale, amaccasahassañcassa sotāpattiphale patiṭṭhāpetvā, tassa anumaggaṃ āgatā sahassitthiparivārā anojādevī āgantvā samīpe nisinnāpi yathā saparisaṃ rājānaṃ na passati, tathā katvā "api, bhante, rājānaṃ passathā"ti vutte "kiṃ pana te rājānaṃ gavesituṃ varaṃ, udāhu attāna"nti?
rows.english: Likewise, after travelling two thousand leagues to meet [King] Mahā Kappina and establishing him in the fruition of non-return and his thousand ministers in the fruition of stream-entry, he so acted that Queen Anojā, who had followed the king with a thousand women attendants and was sitting nearby, did not see the king and his retinue. And when he was asked, “Have you seen the king, venerable sir? ,” he asked, But which is better for you, to seek the king or to seek [your] self? ” (cf. Vin I 23).

rows.pali: "Attānaṃ, bhante"ti vatvā nisinnāya tassā tathā dhammaṃ desesi, yathā sā saddhiṃ itthisahassena sotāpattiphale patiṭṭhāsi, amaccā anāgāmiphale, rājā arahatteti.
rows.english: She replied, “[My] self, venerable sir.” Then he likewise taught her the Dhamma as she sat there, so that, together with the thousand women attendants, she became established in the fruition of stream-entry, while the ministers reached the fruition of non-return, and the king that of Arahantship (see A-a I 322; Dhp-a II 124).

rows.pali: Apica tambapaṇṇidīpaṃ āgatadivase yathā attanā saddhiṃ āgate avasese rājā na passati, evaṃ karontena mahindattherenāpi idaṃ katameva (pārā. aṭṭha. 1.tatiyasaṅgītikathā).
rows.english: 83. Furthermore, this was performed by the Elder Mahinda, who so acted on the day of his arrival in Tambapaṇṇi Island that the king did not see the others who had come with him (see Mahāvaṃsa I 103).

rows.pali: 391.Apica sabbampi pākaṭaṃ pāṭihāriyaṃ āvibhāvaṃ nāma.
rows.english: 84. Furthermore, all miracles of making evident are called an appearance,

rows.pali: Apākaṭapāṭihāriyaṃ tirobhāvaṃ nāma.
rows.english: and all miracles of making unevident are called a vanishing.

rows.pali: Tattha pākaṭapāṭihāriye iddhipi paññāyati iddhimāpi.
rows.english: Herein, in the miracle of making evident, both the supernormal power and the possessor of the supernormal power are displayed.

rows.pali: Taṃ yamakapāṭihāriyena dīpetabbaṃ.
rows.english: That can be illustrated with the Twin Miracle;

rows.pali: Tatra hi "idha tathāgato yamakapāṭihāriyaṃ karoti asādhāraṇaṃ sāvakehi.
rows.english: for in that both are displayed thus: “Here the Perfect One performs the Twin Miracle, which is not shared by disciples.

rows.pali: Uparimakāyato aggikkhandho pavattati, heṭṭhimakāyato udakadhārā pavattatī"ti (paṭi. ma. 1.116) evaṃ ubhayaṃ paññāyittha.
rows.english: He produces a mass of fire from the upper part of his body and a shower of water from the lower part of his body …” (Paṭis I 125).

rows.pali: Apākaṭapāṭihāriye iddhiyeva paññāyati, na iddhimā.
rows.english: In the case of the miracle of making unevident, only the supernormal power is displayed, not the possessor of the supernormal power.

rows.pali: Taṃ mahakasuttena (saṃ. ni. 4.346) ca brahmanimantanikasuttena (ma. ni. 1.501 ādayo) ca dīpetabbaṃ.
rows.english: That can be illustrated by means of the Mahaka Sutta (S IV 200), and the Brahmanimantanika Sutta (M I 330).

rows.pali: Tatra hi āyasmato ca mahakassa, bhagavato ca iddhiyeva paññāyittha, na iddhimā.
rows.english: For there it was only the supernormal power of the venerable Mahaka and of the Blessed One respectively that was displayed, not the possessors of the supernormal power,

rows.pali: Yathāha –
rows.english: according as it is said:

rows.pali: Yathā cāha –
rows.english: 86. Also according as it is said:

rows.pali: 392.Tirokuṭṭaṃ tiropākāraṃ tiropabbataṃ asajjamāno gacchati seyyathāpi ākāseti ettha tirokuṭṭanti parakuṭṭaṃ, kuṭṭassa parabhāganti vuttaṃ hoti.
rows.english: 87. He goes unhindered through walls, through enclosures, through mountains, as though in open space: here through walls is beyond walls; the yonder side of a wall, is what is meant.

rows.pali: Esa nayo itaresu.
rows.english: So with the rest.

rows.pali: Kuṭṭoti ca gehabhittiyā etamadhivacanaṃ.
rows.english: And wall is a term for the wall of a house;

rows.pali: Pākāroti gehavihāragāmādīnaṃ parikkhepapākāro.
rows.english: enclosure is a wall surrounding a house, monastery (park), village, etc.;

rows.pali: Pabbatoti paṃsupabbato vā pāsāṇapabbato vā.
rows.english: mountain is a mountain of soil or a mountain of stone.

rows.pali: Asajjamānoti alaggamāno.
rows.english: Unhindered: not sticking.

rows.pali: Seyyathāpi ākāseti ākāse viya.
rows.english: As though in open space: just as if he were in open space.

rows.pali: Evaṃ gantukāmena pana ākāsakasiṇaṃ samāpajjitvā vuṭṭhāya kuṭṭaṃ vā pākāraṃ vā sinerucakkavāḷesupi aññataraṃ pabbataṃ vā āvajjitvā kataparikammena ākāso hotūti adhiṭṭhātabbo.
rows.english: 88. One who wants to go in this way should attain the space-kasiṇa [jhāna] and emerge, and then do the preliminary work by adverting to the wall or the enclosure or some such mountain as Sineru or the World-sphere Mountains, and he should resolve, “Let there be space.”

rows.pali: Ākāsoyeva hoti.
rows.english: It becomes space only;

rows.pali: Adho otaritukāmassa, uddhaṃ vā ārohitukāmassa susiro hoti, vinivijjhitvā gantukāmassa chiddo.
rows.english: it becomes hollow for him if he wants to go down or up; it becomes cleft for him if he wants to penetrate it.

rows.pali: So tattha asajjamāno gacchati.
rows.english: He goes through it unhindered.

rows.pali: Tipiṭakacūḷābhayatthero panetthāha – "ākāsakasiṇasamāpajjanaṃ, āvuso, kimatthiyaṃ, kiṃ hatthiassādīni abhinimminitukāmo hatthiassādi kasiṇāni samāpajjati, nanu yattha katthaci kasiṇe parikammaṃ katvā aṭṭhasamāpattivasībhāvoyeva pamāṇaṃ.
rows.english: 89. But here the Elder Tipiṭaka Cūḷa-Abhaya said: “Friends, what is the use of attaining the space-kasiṇa [jhāna]? Does one who wants to create elephants, horses, etc., attain an elephant-kasiṇa jhāna or horse-kasiṇa jhāna, and so on? Surely the only standard is mastery in the eight attainments, and after the preliminary work has been done on any kasiṇa,

rows.pali: Yaṃ yaṃ icchati, taṃ tadeva hotī"ti.
rows.english: it then becomes whatever he wishes.”

rows.pali: Bhikkhū āhaṃsu – "pāḷiyā, bhante, ākāsakasiṇaṃyeva āgataṃ, tasmā avassametaṃ vattabba"nti.
rows.english: The bhikkhus said, “Venerable sir, only the space kasiṇa has been given in the text, so it should certainly be mentioned.”

rows.pali: Tatrāyaṃ pāḷi –
rows.english: 90. Here is the text:

rows.pali: Sace panassa bhikkhuno adhiṭṭhahitvā gacchantassa antarā pabbato vā rukkho vā uṭṭheti, kiṃ puna samāpajjitvā adhiṭṭhātabbanti?
rows.english: 91. What if a mountain or a tree is raised in this bhikkhu’s way while he is travelling along after resolving; should he attain and resolve again?

rows.pali: Doso natthi.
rows.english: —There is no harm in that.

rows.pali: Puna samāpajjitvā adhiṭṭhānaṃ hi upajjhāyassa santike nissayaggahaṇasadisaṃ hoti.
rows.english: For attaining and resolving again is like taking the dependence (see Vin I 58; II 274) in the preceptor’s presence.

rows.pali: Iminā ca pana bhikkhunā ākāso hotūti adhiṭṭhitattā ākāso hotiyeva.
rows.english: And because this bhikkhu has resolved, “Let there be space,” there will be only space there,

rows.pali: Purimādhiṭṭhānabaleneva cassa antarā añño pabbato vā rukkho vā utumayo uṭṭhahissatīti aṭṭhānamevetaṃ.
rows.english: and because of the power of his first resolve it is impossible that another mountain or tree can have sprung up meanwhile made by temperature.

rows.pali: Aññena iddhimatā nimmite pana paṭhamanimmānaṃ balavaṃ hoti.
rows.english: However, if it has been created by another possessor of supernormal power and created first, it prevails;

rows.pali: Itarena tassa uddhaṃ vā adho vā gantabbaṃ.
rows.english: the former must go above or below it.

rows.pali: 393.Pathaviyāpi ummujjanimujjanti ettha ummujjanti uṭṭhānaṃ vuccati.
rows.english: 92. He dives in and out of the ground (pathaviyā pi ummujjanimmujjaṃ): here it is rising up that is called “diving out” (ummujja)

rows.pali: Nimujjanti saṃsīdanaṃ.
rows.english: and it is sinking down that is called “diving in” (nimmujja).

rows.pali: Ummujjañca nimujjañca ummujjanimujjaṃ.
rows.english: Ummujjanimmujjaṃ = ummujjañ ca nimmujjañ ca (resolution of compound).

rows.pali: Evaṃ kātukāmena āpokasiṇaṃ samāpajjitvā uṭṭhāya ettake ṭhāne pathavī udakaṃ hotūti paricchinditvā parikammaṃ katvā vuttanayeneva adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ.
rows.english: One who wants to do this should attain the water-kasiṇa [jhāna] and emerge. Then he should do the preliminary work, determining thus, “Let the earth in such an area be water,” and he should resolve in the way already described.

rows.pali: Saha adhiṭṭhānena yathā paricchinne ṭhāne pathavī udakameva hoti.
rows.english: Simultaneously with the resolve, that much extent of earth according as determined becomes water only.

rows.pali: So tattha ummujjanimujjaṃ karoti.
rows.english: It is there he does the diving in and out.

rows.pali: Tatrāyaṃ pāḷi –
rows.english: 93. Here is the text:

rows.pali: Na kevalañca ummujjanimujjameva, nhānapānamukhadhovanabhaṇḍakadhovanādīsu yaṃ yaṃ icchati, taṃ taṃ karoti.
rows.english: 94. And he does not only dive in and out, but whatever else he wants, such as bathing, drinking, mouth washing, washing of chattels, and so on.

rows.pali: Na kevalañca udakameva, sappitelamadhuphāṇitādīsupi yaṃ yaṃ icchati, taṃ taṃ idañcidañca ettakaṃ hotūti āvajjitvā parikammaṃ katvā adhiṭṭhahantassa yathādhiṭṭhitameva hoti.
rows.english: And not only water, but there is whatever else (liquid that) he wants, such as ghee, oil, honey, molasses, and so on. When he does the preliminary work, after adverting thus, “Let there be so much of this and this” and resolves, [396] it becomes as he resolved.

rows.pali: Uddharitvā bhājanagataṃ karontassa sappi sappimeva hoti.
rows.english: If he takes them and fills dishes with them, the ghee is only ghee,

rows.pali: Telādīni telādīniyeva.
rows.english: the oil, etc., only oil, etc.,

rows.pali: Udakaṃ udakameva.
rows.english: the water only water.

rows.pali: So tattha temitukāmova temeti, na temitukāmo na temeti.
rows.english: If he wants to be wetted by it, he is wetted, if he does not want to be wetted by it, he is not wetted.

rows.pali: Tasseva ca sā pathavī udakaṃ hoti sesajanassa pathavīyeva.
rows.english: And it is only for him that that earth becomes water, not for anyone else.

rows.pali: Tattha manussā pattikāpi gacchanti, yānādīhipi gacchanti, kasikammādīnipi karontiyeva.
rows.english: People go on it on foot and in vehicles, etc., and they do their ploughing, etc., there.

rows.pali: Sace panāyaṃ tesampi udakaṃ hotūti icchati, hotiyeva.
rows.english: But if he wishes, “Let it be water for them too,” it becomes water for them too.

rows.pali: Paricchinnakālaṃ pana atikkamitvā yaṃ pakatiyā ghaṭataḷākādīsu udakaṃ, taṃ ṭhapetvā avasesaṃ paricchinnaṭṭhānaṃ pathavīyeva hoti.
rows.english: When the time determined has elapsed, all the extent determined, except for water originally present in water pots, ponds, etc., becomes earth again.

rows.pali: 394.Udakepi abhijjamāneti ettha yaṃ udakaṃ akkamitvā saṃsīdati, taṃ bhijjamānanti vuccati.
rows.english: 95. On unbroken water: here water that one sinks into when trodden on is called “broken,”

rows.pali: Viparītaṃ abhijjamānaṃ.
rows.english: the opposite is called “unbroken.”

rows.pali: Evaṃ gantukāmena pana pathavīkasiṇaṃ samāpajjitvā vuṭṭhāya ettake ṭhāne udakaṃ pathavī hotūti paricchinditvā parikammaṃ katvā vuttanayeneva adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ.
rows.english: But one who wants to go in this way should attain the earth-kasiṇa [jhāna] and emerge. Then he should do the preliminary work, determining thus, “Let the water in such an area become earth,” and he should resolve in the way already described.

rows.pali: Saha adhiṭṭhānena yathā paricchinnaṭṭhāne udakaṃ pathavīyeva hoti.
rows.english: Simultaneously with the resolve, the water in that place becomes earth.

rows.pali: So tattha gacchati, tatrāyaṃ pāḷi –
rows.english: He goes on that. 96. Here is the text:

rows.pali: Na kevalañca gacchati, yaṃ yaṃ iriyāpathaṃ icchati, taṃ taṃ karoti.
rows.english: 97. And he not only goes, but he adopts whatever posture he wishes.

rows.pali: Na kevalañca pathavimeva karoti, maṇisuvaṇṇapabbatarukkhādīsupi yaṃ yaṃ icchati, taṃ taṃ vuttanayeneva āvajjitvā adhiṭṭhāti, yathādhiṭṭhitameva hoti.
rows.english: And not only earth, but whatever else [solid that] he wants such as gems, gold, rocks, trees, etc. he adverts to that and resolves, and it becomes as he resolves.

rows.pali: Tasseva ca taṃ udakaṃ pathavī hoti, sesajanassa udakameva, macchakacchapā ca udakakākādayo ca yathāruci vicaranti.
rows.english: And that water becomes earth only for him; it is water for anyone else. And fishes and turtles and water birds go about there as they like.

rows.pali: Sace panāyaṃ aññesampi manussānaṃ taṃ pathaviṃ kātuṃ icchati, karotiyeva.
rows.english: But if he wishes to make it earth for other people, he does so too.

rows.pali: Paricchinnakālātikkamena pana udakameva hoti.
rows.english: When the time determined has elapsed, it becomes water again.

rows.pali: 395.Pallaṅkena kamatīti pallaṅkena gacchati.
rows.english: 98. Seated cross-legged he travels: he goes seated cross-legged.

rows.pali: Pakkhī sakuṇoti pakkhehi yuttasakuṇo.
rows.english: Like a winged bird: like a bird furnished with wings.

rows.pali: Evaṃ kātukāmena pana pathavīkasiṇaṃ samāpajjitvā vuṭṭhāya sace nisinno gantumicchati, pallaṅkappamāṇaṃ ṭhānaṃ paricchinditvā parikammaṃ katvā vuttanayeneva adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ.
rows.english: One who wants to do this should attain the earth kasiṇa and emerge. Then if he wants to go cross-legged, he should do the preliminary work and determine an area the size of a seat for sitting cross-legged on, and he should resolve in the way already described.

rows.pali: Sace nipanno gantukāmo hoti mañcappamāṇaṃ, sace padasā gantukāmo hoti maggappamāṇanti evaṃ yathānurūpaṃ ṭhānaṃ paricchinditvā vuttanayeneva pathavī hotūti adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ, saha adhiṭṭhānena pathavīyeva hoti.
rows.english: If he wants to go lying down, he determines an area the size of a bed. If he wants to go on foot, he determines a suitable area the size of a path, and he resolves in the way already described: “Let it be earth.” Simultaneously with the resolve it becomes earth.

rows.pali: Tatrāyaṃ pāḷi –
rows.english: 99. Here is the text:

rows.pali: Ākāse gantukāmena ca bhikkhunā dibbacakkhulābhināpi bhavitabbaṃ.
rows.english: 100.And a bhikkhu who wants to travel in space should be an obtainer of the divine eye.

rows.pali: Kasmā?
rows.english: Why?

rows.pali: Antare utusamuṭṭhānā vā pabbatarukkhādayo honti, nāgasupaṇṇādayo vā usūyantā māpenti, nesaṃ dassanatthaṃ.
rows.english: On the way there may be mountains, trees, etc., that are temperature-originated, or jealous nāgas, supaṇṇas, etc., may create them. He will need to be able to see these.

rows.pali: Te pana disvā kiṃ kātabbanti?
rows.english: But what should be done on seeing them?

rows.pali: Pādakajjhānaṃ samāpajjitvā vuṭṭhāya ākāso hotūti parikammaṃ katvā adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ.
rows.english: He should attain the basic jhāna and emerge, and then he should do the preliminary work thus, “Let there be space,” and resolve.

rows.pali: Thero panāha "samāpattisamāpajjanaṃ, āvuso, kimatthiyaṃ, nanu samāhitamevassa cittaṃ, tena yaṃ yaṃ ṭhānaṃ ākāso hotūti adhiṭṭhāti, ākāsoyeva hotī"ti.
rows.english: 101.But the Elder [Tipiṭaka Cūḷa-Abhaya] said: “Friends, what is the use of attaining the attainment? Is not his mind concentrated? Hence any area that he has resolved thus, ‘Let it be space’ is space.”

rows.pali: Kiñcāpi evamāha, atha kho tirokuṭṭapārihāriye vuttanayeneva paṭipajjitabbaṃ.
rows.english: Though he spoke thus, nevertheless the matter should be treated as described under the miracle of going unhindered through walls.

rows.pali: Apica okāse orohaṇatthampi iminā dibbacakkhulābhinā bhavitabbaṃ, ayañhi sace anokāse nhānatitthe vā gāmadvāre vā orohati.
rows.english: Moreover, he should be an obtainer of the divine eye for the purpose of descending in a secluded place, for if he descends in a public place, in a bathing place, or at a village gate,

rows.pali: Mahājanassa pākaṭo hoti.
rows.english: he is exposed to the multitude.

rows.pali: Tasmā dibbacakkhunā passitvā anokāsaṃ vajjetvā okāse otaratīti.
rows.english: So, seeing with the divine eye, he should avoid a place where there is no open space and descend in an open space.

rows.pali: 396.Imepi candimasūriye evaṃmahiddhike evaṃmahānubhāve pāṇinā parāmasati parimajjatīti ettha candimasūriyānaṃ dvācattālīsayojanasahassassa upari caraṇena mahiddhikatā, tīsu dīpesu ekakkhaṇe ālokakaraṇena mahānubhāvatā veditabbā.
rows.english: 102.With his hand he touches and strokes the moon and sun so mighty and powerful: here the “might” of the moon and sun should be understood to consist in the fact that they travel at an altitude of forty-two thousand leagues, and their “power” to consist in their simultaneous illuminating of three [of the four] continents.

rows.pali: Evaṃ upari caraṇaālokakaraṇehi vā mahiddhike teneva mahānubhāve.
rows.english: Or they are “mighty” because they travel overhead and give light as they do, and they are “powerful” because of that same might.

rows.pali: Parāmasatīti pariggaṇhati ekadese vā chupati.
rows.english: He touches: he seizes, or he touches in one place.

rows.pali: Parimajjatīti samantato ādāsatalaṃ viya parimajjati.
rows.english: Strokes: he strokes all over, as if it were the surface of a looking-glass.

rows.pali: Ayaṃ panassa iddhi abhiññāpādakajjhānavaseneva ijjhati, natthettha kasiṇasamāpattiniyamo.
rows.english: 103.This supernormal power is successful simply through the jhāna that is made the basis for direct-knowledge; there is no special kasiṇa attainment here.

rows.pali: Vuttañhetaṃ paṭisambhidāyaṃ –
rows.english: For this is said in the Paṭisambhidā:

rows.pali: Svāyaṃ yadi icchati gantvā parāmasituṃ, gantvā parāmasati, yadi pana idheva nisinnako vā nipannako vā parāmasitukāmo hoti, hatthapāse hotūti adhiṭṭhāti, adhiṭṭhānabalena vaṇṭā muttatālaphalaṃ viya āgantvā hatthapāse ṭhite vā parāmasati, hatthaṃ vā vaḍḍhetvā.
rows.english: Then he either touches them as they stand within hand’s reach when they have come by the power of the resolve like palmyra fruits loosened from their stalk, or he does so by enlarging his hand.

rows.pali: Vaḍḍhentassa pana kiṃ upādiṇṇakaṃ vaḍḍhati, anupādiṇṇakanti?
rows.english: But when he enlarges his hand, does he enlarge what is clung to or what is not clung to?

rows.pali: Upādiṇṇakaṃ nissāya anupādiṇṇakaṃ vaḍḍhati.
rows.english: He enlarges what is not clung to supported by what is clung to.

rows.pali: Tattha tipiṭakacūḷanāgatthero āha "kiṃ panāvuso, upādiṇṇakaṃ khuddakampi mahantampi na hoti, nanu yadā bhikkhu tālacchiddādīhi nikkhamati, tadā upādiṇṇakaṃ khuddakaṃ hoti.
rows.english: 105.Here the Elder Tipiṭaka Cūḷa-Nāga said: “But, friends, why does what is clung to not become small and big too? When a bhikkhu comes out through a keyhole, does not what is clung to become small?

rows.pali: Yadā mahantaṃ attabhāvaṃ karoti, tadā mahantaṃ hoti mahāmoggallānattherassa viyā"ti.
rows.english: And when he makes his body big, does it not then become big, as in the case of the Elder Mahā Moggallāna? ”

rows.pali: Ekasmiṃ kira samaye anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati bhagavato dhammadesanaṃ sutvā "sve, bhante, pañcahi bhikkhusatehi saddhiṃ amhākaṃ gehe bhikkhaṃ gaṇhathā"ti nimantetvā pakkami.
rows.english: 106.At one time, it seems, when the householder Anāthapiṇḍika had heard the Blessed One preaching the Dhamma, he invited him thus, Venerable sir, take alms at our house together with five hundred bhikkhus,” and then he departed.

rows.pali: Bhagavā adhivāsetvā taṃdivasāvasesaṃ rattibhāgañca vītināmetvā paccūsasamaye dasasahassilokadhātuṃ olokesi.
rows.english: The Blessed One consented. When the rest of that day and part of the night had passed, he surveyed the ten-thousandfold world element in the early morning.

rows.pali: Athassa nandopanando nāma nāgarājā ñāṇamukhe āpāthamāgacchi.
rows.english: Then the royal nāga (serpent) called Nandopananda came within the range of his knowledge.

rows.pali: Bhagavā "ayaṃ nāgarājā mayhaṃ ñāṇamukhe āpāthamāgacchi, atthi nu kho assa upanissayo"ti āvajjento "ayaṃ micchādiṭṭhiko tīsu ratanesu appasannoti disvā ko nu kho imaṃ micchādiṭṭhito viveceyyā"ti āvajjento mahāmoggallānattheraṃ addasa.
rows.english: 107.The Blessed One considered him thus: “This royal nāga has come into the range of my knowledge. Has he the potentiality for development? ” Then he saw that he had wrong view and no confidence in the Three Jewels. He considered thus, “Who is there that can cure him of his wrong view? ” He saw that the Elder Mahā Moggallāna could.

rows.pali: Tato pabhātāya rattiyā sarīrapaṭijagganaṃ katvā āyasmantaṃ ānandaṃ āmantesi – "ānanda, pañcannaṃ bhikkhusatānaṃ ārocehi tathāgato devacārikaṃ gacchatī"ti.
rows.english: Then when the night had turned to dawn, after he had seen to the needs of the body, he addressed the venerable Ānanda: “Ānanda, tell five hundred bhikkhus that the Perfect One is going on a visit to the gods. ”

rows.pali: Taṃ divasañca nandopanandassa āpānabhūmiṃ sajjayiṃsu.
rows.english: 108.It was on that day that they had got a banqueting place ready for Nandopananda.

rows.pali: So dibbaratanapallaṅke dibbena setacchattena dhāriyamānena tividhanāṭakehi ceva nāgaparisāya ca parivuto dibbabhājanesu upaṭṭhāpitaṃ annapānavidhiṃ olokayamāno nisinno hoti.
rows.english: He was sitting on a divine couch with a divine white parasol held aloft, surrounded by the three kinds of dancers18 and a retinue of nāgas, and surveying the various kinds of food and drink served up in divine vessels.

rows.pali: Atha bhagavā yathā nāgarājā passati, tathā katvā tassa vitānamatthakeneva pañcahi bhikkhusatehi saddhiṃ tāvatiṃsadevalokābhimukho pāyāsi.
rows.english: Then the Blessed One so acted that the royal nāga saw him as he proceeded directly above his canopy in the direction of the divine world of the Thirty-three, accompanied by the five hundred bhikkhus.

rows.pali: Tena kho pana samayena nandopanandassa nāgarājassa evarūpaṃ pāpakaṃ diṭṭhigataṃ uppannaṃ hoti – "ime hi nāma muṇḍakā samaṇakā amhākaṃ uparūparibhavanena devānaṃ tāvatiṃsānaṃ bhavanaṃ pavisantipi nikkhamantipi, na dāni ito paṭṭhāya imesaṃ amhākaṃ matthake pādapaṃsuṃ okirantānaṃ gantuṃ dassāmī"ti uṭṭhāya sinerupādaṃ gantvā taṃ attabhāvaṃ vijahitvā sineruṃ sattakkhattuṃ bhogehi parikkhipitvā upari phaṇaṃ katvā tāvatiṃsabhavanaṃ avakujjena phaṇena gahetvā adassanaṃ gamesi.
rows.english: 109.Then this evil view arose in Nandopananda the royal nāga: “There go these bald-headed monks in and out of the realm of the Thirty-three directly over my realm. I will not have them scattering the dirt off their feet on our heads. ” He got up, and he went to the foot of Sineru. Changing his form, he surrounded it seven times with his coils. Then he spread his hood over the realm of the Thirty- three and made everything there invisible.

rows.pali: Atha kho āyasmā raṭṭhapālo bhagavantaṃ etadavoca "pubbe, bhante, imasmiṃ padese ṭhito sineruṃ passāmi, sineruparibhaṇḍaṃ passāmi, tāvatiṃsaṃ passāmi, vejayantaṃ passāmi, vejayantassa pāsādassa upari dhajaṃ passāmi.
rows.english: 110.The venerable Raṭṭhapāla said to the Blessed One: “Venerable sir, standing in this place formerly I used to see Sineru and the ramparts of Sineru,19 and the Thirty-three, and the Vejayanta Palace, and the flag over the Vejayanta Palace.

rows.pali: Ko nu kho, bhante, hetu ko paccayo, yaṃ etarahi neva sineruṃ passāmi - pe - na vejayantassa pāsādassa upari dhajaṃ passāmī"ti.
rows.english: Venerable sir, what is the cause, what is the reason, why I now see neither Sineru nor … the flag over the Vejayanta Palace?”

rows.pali: "Ayaṃ, raṭṭhapāla, nandopanando nāma nāgarājā tumhākaṃ kupito sineruṃ sattakkhattuṃ bhogehi parikkhipitvā upari phaṇena paṭicchādetvā andhakāraṃ katvā ṭhito"ti.
rows.english: —“This royal nāga called Nandopananda is angry with us, Raṭṭhapāla. He has surrounded Sineru seven times with his coils, and he stands there covering us with his raised hood, making it dark.”

rows.pali: "Damemi naṃ, bhante"ti.
rows.english: —“I will tame him, venerable sir.”

rows.pali: Na bhagavā anujāni.
rows.english: But the Blessed One would not allow it.

rows.pali: Atha kho āyasmā bhaddiyo āyasmā rāhuloti anukkamena sabbepi bhikkhū uṭṭhahiṃsu.
rows.english: Then the venerable Bhaddiya and the venerable Rāhula and all the bhikkhus in turn offered to do so,

rows.pali: Na bhagavā anujāni.
rows.english: but the Blessed One would not allow it.

rows.pali: Avasāne mahāmoggallānatthero "ahaṃ, bhante, damemi na"nti āha.
rows.english: 111.Last of all the venerable Mahā Moggallāna said, “I will tame him, venerable sir.”

rows.pali: "Damehi moggallānā"ti bhagavā anujāni.
rows.english: The Blessed One allowed it, saying, “Tame him, Moggallāna.”

rows.pali: Thero attabhāvaṃ vijahitvā mahantaṃ nāgarājavaṇṇaṃ abhinimminitvā nandopanandaṃ cuddasakkhattuṃ bhogehi parikkhipitvā tassa phaṇamatthake attano phaṇaṃ ṭhapetvā sinerunā saddhiṃ abhinippīḷesi.
rows.english: The elder abandoned that form and assumed the form of a huge royal nāga, and he surrounded Nandopananda fourteen times with his coils and raised his hood above the other’s hood, and he squeezed him against Sineru.

rows.pali: Nāgarājā padhūmāyi.
rows.english: The royal nāga produced smoke.

rows.pali: Theropi na tuyhaṃyeva sarīre dhūmo atthi, mayhampi atthīti padhūmāyi.
rows.english: The elder said, “There is smoke not only in your body but also in mine,” and he produced smoke.

rows.pali: Nāgarājassa dhūmo theraṃ na bādhati.
rows.english: The royal nāga’s smoke did not distress the elder,

rows.pali: Therassa pana dhūmo nāgarājānaṃ bādhati.
rows.english: but the elder’s smoke distressed the royal nāga.

rows.pali: Tato nāgarājā pajjali.
rows.english: Then the royal nāga produced flames.

rows.pali: Theropi na tuyhaṃyeva sarīre aggi atthi, mayhampi atthīti pajjali.
rows.english: The elder said, “There is fire not only in your body but also in mine,” and he produced flames.

rows.pali: Nāgarājassa tejo theraṃ na bādhati.
rows.english: The royal nāga’s fire did not distress the elder,

rows.pali: Therassa pana tejo nāgarājānaṃ bādhati.
rows.english: but the elder’s fire distressed the royal nāga.

rows.pali: Nāgarājā ayaṃ maṃ sinerunā abhinippīḷetvā dhūmāyati ceva pajjalati cāti cintetvā "bho tvaṃ kosī"ti paṭipucchi.
rows.english: 112. The royal nāga thought, “He has squeezed me against Sineru, and he has produced both smoke and flames. ” Then he asked, “Sir, who are you?”

rows.pali: "Ahaṃ kho, nanda, moggallāno"ti.
rows.english: —“I am Moggallāna, Nanda.”

rows.pali: "Bhante, attano bhikkhubhāvena tiṭṭhāhī"ti.
rows.english: —“Venerable sir, resume your proper bhikkhu’s state.”

rows.pali: Thero taṃ attabhāvaṃ vijahitvā tassa dakkhiṇakaṇṇasotena pavisitvā vāmakaṇṇasotena nikkhami, vāmakaṇṇasotena pavisitvā dakkhiṇakaṇṇasotena nikkhami, tathā dakkhiṇanāsasotena pavisitvā vāmanāsasotena nikkhami, vāmanāsasotena pavisitvā dakkhiṇanāsasotena nikkhami.
rows.english: The elder abandoned that form, and he went into his right ear and came out from his left ear; then he went into his left ear and came out from his right ear. Likewise he went into his right nostril and came out from his left nostril; then he went into his left nostril and came out from his right nostril.

rows.pali: Tato nāgarājā mukhaṃ vivari.
rows.english: Then the royal nāga opened his mouth.

rows.pali: Thero mukhena pavisitvā antokucchiyaṃ pācīnena ca pacchimena ca caṅkamati.
rows.english: The elder went inside it, and he walked up and down, east and west, inside his belly.

rows.pali: Bhagavā "moggallāna, manasikarohi mahiddhiko esa nāgo"ti āha.
rows.english: 113.The Blessed One said, “Moggallāna, Moggallāna, beware; this is a mighty nāga.”

rows.pali: Thero "mayhaṃ kho, bhante, cattāro iddhipādā bhāvitā bahulīkatā yānīkatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhā, tiṭṭhatu, bhante, nandopanando, ahaṃ nandopanandasadisānaṃ nāgarājānaṃ satampi sahassampi satasahassampi dameyya"nti āha.
rows.english: The elder said, “Venerable sir, the four roads to power have been developed by me, repeatedly practiced, made the vehicle, made the basis, established, consolidated, and properly undertaken. I can tame not only Nandopananda, venerable sir, but a hundred, a thousand, a hundred thousand royal nāgas like Nandopananda. ”

rows.pali: Nāgarājā cintesi "pavisanto tāva me na diṭṭho, nikkhamanakāle dāni naṃ dāṭhantare pakkhipitvā saṅkhādissāmī"ti cintetvā nikkhama bhante, mā maṃ antokucchiyaṃ aparāparaṃ caṅkamanto bādhayitthāti āha.
rows.english: 114.The royal nāga thought, “When he went in the first place I did not see him. But now when he comes out I shall catch him between my fangs and chew him up. ” Then he said, “Venerable sir, come out. Do not keep troubling me by walking up and down inside my belly.”

rows.pali: Thero nikkhamitvā bahi aṭṭhāsi.
rows.english: The elder came out and stood outside.

rows.pali: Nāgarājā ayaṃ soti disvā nāsavātaṃ vissajji.
rows.english: The royal nāga recognized him, and blew a blast from his nose.

rows.pali: Thero catutthaṃ jhānaṃ samāpajji.
rows.english: The elder attained the fourth jhāna,

rows.pali: Lomakūpampissa vāto cāletuṃ nāsakkhi.
rows.english: and the blast failed to move even a single hair on his body.

rows.pali: Avasesā bhikkhū kira ādito paṭṭhāya sabbapāṭihāriyāni kātuṃ sakkuṇeyyuṃ, imaṃ pana ṭhānaṃ patvā evaṃ khippanisantino hutvā samāpajjituṃ na sakkhissantīti tesaṃ bhagavā nāgarājadamanaṃ nānujāni.
rows.english: The other bhikkhus would, it seems, have been able to perform all the miracles up to now, but at this point they could not have attained with so rapid a response, which is why the Blessed One would not allow them to tame the royal nāga.

rows.pali: Nāgarājā "ahaṃ imassa samaṇassa nāsavātena lomakūpampi cāletuṃ nāsakkhiṃ, mahiddhiko samaṇo"ti cintesi.
rows.english: 115. The royal nāga thought, “I have been unable to move even a single hair on this monk’s body with the blast from my nose. He is a mighty monk.”

rows.pali: Thero attabhāvaṃ vijahitvā supaṇṇarūpaṃ abhinimminitvā supaṇṇavātaṃ dassento nāgarājānaṃ anubandhi.
rows.english: The elder abandoned that form, and having assumed the form of a supaṇṇa, he pursued the royal nāga demonstrating the supaṇṇa’s blast.

rows.pali: Nāgarājā taṃ attabhāvaṃ vijahitvā māṇavakavaṇṇaṃ abhinimminitvā "bhante, tumhākaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmī"ti vadanto therassa pāde vandi.
rows.english: The royal nāga abandoned that form, and having assumed the form of a young brahman, he said, “Venerable sir, I go for refuge to you,” and he paid homage at the elder’s feet.

rows.pali: Thero "satthā, nanda, āgato, ehi gamissāmā"ti nāgarājānaṃ damayitvā nibbisaṃ katvā gahetvā bhagavato santikaṃ agamāsi.
rows.english: The elder said, “The Master has come, Nanda; come, let us go to him. ” So having tamed the royal nāga and deprived him of his poison, he went with him to the Blessed One’s presence.

rows.pali: Nāgarājā bhagavantaṃ vanditvā "bhante, tumhākaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmī"ti āha.
rows.english: 116.The royal nāga paid homage to the Blessed One and said, “Venerable sir, I go for refuge to you.”

rows.pali: Bhagavā "sukhī hohi, nāgarājā"ti vatvā bhikkhusaṅghaparivuto anāthapiṇḍikassa nivesanaṃ agamāsi.
rows.english: The Blessed One said, “May you be happy, royal nāga. ” Then he went, followed by the Community of Bhikkhus, to Anāthapiṇḍika’s house.

rows.pali: Anāthapiṇḍiko "kiṃ, bhante, atidivā āgatatthā"ti āha.
rows.english: Anāthapiṇḍika said, “Venerable sir, why have you come so late?”

rows.pali: Moggallānassa ca nandopanandassa ca saṅgāmo ahosīti.
rows.english: —“There was a battle between Moggallāna and Nandopananda.”

rows.pali: Kassa, bhante, jayo, kassa parājayoti.
rows.english: —“Who won, venerable sir? Who was defeated?”

rows.pali: Moggallānassa jayo, nandassa parājayoti.
rows.english: —“Moggallāna won; Nanda was defeated.”

rows.pali: Anāthapiṇḍiko "adhivāsetu me, bhante, bhagavā sattāhaṃ ekapaṭipāṭiyā bhattaṃ, sattāhaṃ therassa sakkāraṃ karissāmī"ti vatvā sattāhaṃ buddhapamukhānaṃ pañcannaṃ bhikkhusatānaṃ mahāsakkāraṃ akāsi.
rows.english: Anāthapiṇḍika said, “Venerable sir, let the Blessed One consent to my providing meals for seven days in a single series, and to my honouring the elder for seven days. ” Then for seven days he accorded great honour to the five hundred bhikkhus with the Enlightened One at their head.

rows.pali: Iti imaṃ imasmiṃ nandopanandadamane kataṃ mahantaṃ attabhāvaṃ sandhāyetaṃ vuttaṃ "yadā mahantaṃ attabhāvaṃ karoti, tadā mahantaṃ hoti mahāmoggallānattherassa viyā"ti.
rows.english: 117.So it was with reference to this enlarged form created during this taming of Nandopananda that it was said: “When he makes his body big, does it not then become big, as in the case of the Elder Mahā Moggallāna? ” (§105).

rows.pali: Evaṃ vuttepi bhikkhū upādiṇṇakaṃ nissāya anupādiṇṇakameva vaḍḍhatīti āhaṃsu.
rows.english: Although this was said, the bhikkhus observed, “He enlarges only what is not clung to supported by what is clung to.”

rows.pali: Ayameva cettha yutti.
rows.english: And only this is correct here. 20

rows.pali: So evaṃ katvā na kevalaṃ candimasūriye parāmasati.
rows.english: 118.And when he has done this, he not only touches the moon and sun,

rows.pali: Sace icchati pādakathalikaṃ katvā pāde ṭhapeti, pīṭhaṃ katvā nisīdati, mañcaṃ katvā nipajjati, apassenaphalakaṃ katvā apassayati.
rows.english: but if he wishes, he makes a footstool [of them] and puts his feet on it, he makes a chair [of them] and sits on it, he makes a bed [of them] and lies on it, he makes a leaning-plank [of them] and leans on it.

rows.pali: Yathā ca eko, evaṃ aparopi.
rows.english: And as one does, so do others.

rows.pali: Anekesupi hi bhikkhusatasahassesu evaṃ karontesu tesañca ekamekassa tatheva ijjhati.
rows.english: For even when several hundred thousand bhikkhus do this and each one succeeds,

rows.pali: Candimasūriyānañca gamanampi ālokakaraṇampi tatheva hoti.
rows.english: still the motions of the moon and sun and their radiance remain the same.

rows.pali: Yathā hi pātisahassesu udakapūresu sabbapātīsu ca candamaṇḍalāni dissanti.
rows.english: For just as when a thousand saucers are full of water and moon disks are seen in all the saucers,

rows.pali: Pākatikameva ca candassa gamanaṃ ālokakaraṇañca hoti.
rows.english: still the moon’s motion is normal and so is its radiance.

rows.pali: Tathūpamametaṃ pāṭihāriyaṃ.
rows.english: And this miracle resembles that.

rows.pali: 397.Yāva brahmalokāpīti brahmalokampi paricchedaṃ katvā.
rows.english: 119.Even as far as the Brahmā-world: having made even the Brahmā-world the limit.

rows.pali: Kāyena vasaṃ vattetīti tattha brahmaloke kāyena attano vasaṃ vatteti.
rows.english: He wields bodily mastery: herein, he wields self-mastery in the Brahmā- world by means of the body.

rows.pali: Tassattho pāḷiṃ anugantvā veditabbo.
rows.english: The meaning of this should be understood according to the text.

rows.pali: Ayañhettha pāḷi –
rows.english: Here is the text:

rows.pali: Tattha dūrepi santike adhiṭṭhātīti pādakajjhānato vuṭṭhāya dūre devalokaṃ vā brahmalokaṃ vā āvajjati santike hotūti.
rows.english: 120.Herein, though far, he resolves upon nearness: having emerged from the basic jhāna, he adverts to a far-off world of the gods or to the Brahmā-world thus, “Let it be near.”

rows.pali: Āvajjitvā parikammaṃ katvā puna samāpajjitvā ñāṇena adhiṭṭhāti santike hotūti, santike hoti.
rows.english: Having adverted and done the preliminary work, he attains again, and then resolves with knowledge: “Let it be near.” It becomes near.

rows.pali: Esa nayo sesapadesupi.
rows.english: The same method of explanation applies to the other clauses too.

rows.pali: Tattha ko dūraṃ gahetvā santikaṃ akāsīti?
rows.english: 121.Herein, who has taken what was far and made it near?

rows.pali: Bhagavā.
rows.english: The Blessed One.

rows.pali: Bhagavā hi yamakapāṭihāriyāvasāne devalokaṃ gacchanto yugandharañca sineruñca santike katvā pathavītalato ekapādaṃ yugandhare patiṭṭhapetvā dutiyaṃ sinerumatthake ṭhapesi.
rows.english: For when the Blessed One was going to the divine world after the Twin Miracle, he made Yugandhara and Sineru near, and from the earth’s surface he set one foot [403] on Yugandhara, and then he set the other on the summit of Sineru.

rows.pali: Añño ko akāsi?
rows.english: 122.Who else has done it?

rows.pali: Mahāmoggallānatthero.
rows.english: The Elder Mahā Moggallāna.

rows.pali: Thero hi sāvatthito bhattakiccaṃ katvā nikkhantaṃ dvādasayojanikaṃ parisaṃ tiṃsayojanaṃ saṅkassanagaramaggaṃ saṅkhipitvā taṅkhaṇaññeva sampāpesi.
rows.english: For when the elder was leaving Sāvatthī after completing his meal, he abridged the twelve-league crowd and the thirty-league road to the city of Saṅkassa, and he arrived at the same moment.

rows.pali: Apica tambapaṇṇidīpe cūḷasamuddattheropi akāsi.
rows.english: 123.Furthermore, the Elder Cūḷa Samudda did it as well in Tambapaṇṇi Island.

rows.pali: Dubbhikkhasamaye kira therassa santikaṃ pātova satta bhikkhusatāni āgamaṃsu.
rows.english: During a time of scarcity, it seems, seven hundred bhikkhus came to the elder one morning.

rows.pali: Thero "mahā bhikkhusaṅgho kuhiṃ bhikkhācāro bhavissatī"ti cintento sakalatambapaṇṇidīpe adisvā "paratīre pāṭaliputte bhavissatī"ti disvā bhikkhū pattacīvaraṃ gāhāpetvā "ethāvuso, bhikkhācāraṃ gamissāmā"ti pathaviṃ saṅkhipitvā pāṭaliputtaṃ gato.
rows.english: The elder thought, “Where can a large community of bhikkhus wander for alms?” He saw nowhere at all in Tambapaṇṇi Island, but he saw that it would be possible on the other shore at Pāṭaliputta (Patna). He got the bhikkhus to take their bowls and [outer] robes, and he said, “Come friends, let us go wandering for alms.” Then he abridged the earth and went to Pāṭaliputta.

rows.pali: Bhikkhū "kataraṃ, bhante, imaṃ nagara"nti pucchiṃsu.
rows.english: The bhikkhus asked, “What is the city, venerable sir?”

rows.pali: Pāṭaliputtaṃ, āvusoti.
rows.english: —“It is Pāṭaliputta, friends.”

rows.pali: Pāṭaliputtaṃ nāma dūre bhanteti.
rows.english: — “Pāṭaliputta is far away, venerable sir.”

rows.pali: Āvuso, mahallakattherā nāma dūrepi gahetvā santike karontīti.
rows.english: —“Friends, experienced elders make what is far near.”

rows.pali: Mahāsamuddo kuhiṃ, bhanteti?
rows.english: —“Where is the ocean (mahā-samudda), venerable sir?”—

rows.pali: Nanu, āvuso, antarā ekaṃ nīlamātikaṃ atikkamitvā āgatatthāti?
rows.english: “Friends, did you not cross a blue stream on the way as you came?”

rows.pali: Āma, bhante.
rows.english: —“Yes, venerable sir,

rows.pali: Mahāsamuddo pana mahantoti.
rows.english: but the ocean is vast.”

rows.pali: Āvuso, mahallakattherā nāma mahantampi khuddakaṃ karontīti.
rows.english: —“Friends, experienced elders also make what is vast small.”

rows.pali: Yathā cāyaṃ, evaṃ tissadattattheropi sāyanhasamaye nhāyitvā katuttarāsaṅgo mahābodhiṃ vandissāmīti citte uppanne santike akāsi.
rows.english: 124.And the Elder Tissadatta did likewise, when he had put on his upper robes after bathing in the evening, and the thought of paying homage at the Great Enlightenment Tree arose in him.

rows.pali: Santikaṃ pana gahetvā ko dūramakāsīti?
rows.english: 125.Who has taken what was near and made it far?

rows.pali: Bhagavā.
rows.english: The Blessed One.

rows.pali: Bhagavā hi attano ca aṅgulimālassa (ma. ni. 2.348) ca antaraṃ santikampi dūramakāsīti.
rows.english: For although Aṅgulimāla was near to the Blessed One, yet he made him far (see M II 99).

rows.pali: Atha ko bahukaṃ thokaṃ akāsīti?
rows.english: 126.Who has made much little?

rows.pali: Mahākassapatthero.
rows.english: The Elder Mahā Kassapa.

rows.pali: Rājagahe kira nakkhattadivase pañcasatā kumāriyo candapūve gahetvā nakkhattakīḷanatthāya gacchantiyo bhagavantaṃ disvā kiñci nādaṃsu.
rows.english: One feast day at Rājagaha, it seems, there were five hundred girls on their way to enjoy the festival, and they had taken moon cakes with them. They saw the Blessed One but gave him nothing.

rows.pali: Pacchato āgacchantaṃ pana theraṃ disvā amhākaṃ thero eti pūvaṃ dassāmāti sabbā pūve gahetvā theraṃ upasaṅkamiṃsu.
rows.english: On their way back, however, they saw the elder. Thinking, “He is our elder,” they each took a cake and approached the elder.

rows.pali: Thero pattaṃ nīharitvā sabbaṃ ekapattapūramattamakāsi.
rows.english: The elder took out his bowl and made a single bowlful of them all.

rows.pali: Bhagavā theraṃ āgamayamāno purato nisīdi.
rows.english: The Blessed One had sat down first to await the elder.

rows.pali: Thero āharitvā bhagavato adāsi.
rows.english: The elder brought them and gave them to the Blessed One.

rows.pali: Illisaseṭṭhivatthusmiṃ pana mahāmoggallānatthero thokaṃ bahukamakāsi, kākavaliyavatthusmiñca bhagavā.
rows.english: 127.In the story of the rich man Illīsa, however, (J-a I 348; Dhp-a I 372) the Elder Mahā Moggallāna made little much. And in the story of Kākavaḷiya the Blessed One did so.

rows.pali: Mahākassapatthero kira sattāhaṃ samāpattiyā vītināmetvā daliddasaṅgahaṃ karonto kākavaliyassa nāma duggatamanussassa gharadvāre aṭṭhāsi.
rows.english: The Elder Mahā Kassapa, it seems, after spending seven days in attainment, stood at the house door of a man in poor circumstances called Kākavaḷiya in order to show favour to the poor.

rows.pali: Tassa jāyā theraṃ disvā patino pakkaṃ aloṇambilayāguṃ patte ākiri.
rows.english: His wife saw the elder, and she poured into his bowl the unsalted sour gruel that she had cooked for her husband.

rows.pali: Thero taṃ gahetvā bhagavato hatthe ṭhapesi.
rows.english: The elder took it and placed it in the Blessed One’s hand.

rows.pali: Bhagavā mahābhikkhusaṅghassa pahonakaṃ katvā adhiṭṭhāsi.
rows.english: The Blessed One resolved to make it enough for the Greater Community of Bhikkhus.

rows.pali: Ekapattena ābhatā sabbesaṃ pahosi.
rows.english: What was brought in a single bowl became enough for all.

rows.pali: Kākavaliyopi sattame divase seṭṭhiṭṭhānaṃ alatthāti.
rows.english: And on the seventh day Kākavaḷiya became a rich man.

rows.pali: Na kevalañca thokassa bahukaraṇaṃ, madhuraṃ amadhuraṃ, amadhuraṃ madhurantiādīsupi yaṃ yaṃ icchati, sabbaṃ iddhimato ijjhati.
rows.english: 128.And not only in the case of making little much, but whatever the possessor of supernormal power wishes, whether to make the sweet unsweet, etc., it is successful for him.

rows.pali: Tathā hi mahāanuḷatthero nāma sambahule bhikkhū piṇḍāya caritvā sukkhabhattameva labhitvā gaṅgātīre nisīditvā paribhuñjamāne disvā gaṅgāya udakaṃ sappimaṇḍanti adhiṭṭhahitvā sāmaṇerānaṃ saññaṃ adāsi.
rows.english: For so it was that when the Elder Mahā Anula saw many bhikkhus sitting on the banks of the Gaṅgā River [in Sri Lanka] eating plain rice, which was all that they had got after doing their alms round, he resolved, “Let the Gaṅgā River water be cream of ghee,” and he gave a sign to the novices.

rows.pali: Te thālakehi āharitvā bhikkhusaṅghassa adaṃsu.
rows.english: They fetched it in their vessels and gave it to the Community of Bhikkhus.

rows.pali: Sabbe madhurena sappimaṇḍena bhuñjiṃsūti.
rows.english: All of them ate their meal with sweet cream of ghee.

rows.pali: Dibbenacakkhunāti idheva ṭhito ālokaṃ vaḍḍhetvā tassa brahmuno rūpaṃ passati.
rows.english: 129.With the divine eye: remaining here and extending light, he sees the visible form of that Brahmā.

rows.pali: Idheva ca ṭhito sabbaṃ tassa bhāsato saddaṃ suṇāti.
rows.english: And remaining here he also hears the sound of his speech

rows.pali: Cittaṃ pajānāti.
rows.english: and he understands his mind.

rows.pali: Kāyavasena cittaṃ pariṇāmetīti karajakāyassa vasena cittaṃ pariṇāmeti.
rows.english: 130.He converts his mind according to his body: he converts the mind to accord with the material body;

rows.pali: Pādakajjhānacittaṃ gahetvā kāye āropeti.
rows.english: taking the consciousness of the basic jhāna, he mounts it upon the body,

rows.pali: Kāyānugatikaṃ karoti dandhagamanaṃ.
rows.english: he makes its going slow to coincide with that of the body;

rows.pali: Kāyagamanaṃ hi dandhaṃ hoti.
rows.english: for the body’s mode of going is slow.

rows.pali: Sukhasaññañca lahusaññañca okkamatīti pādakajjhānārammaṇena iddhicittena sahajātaṃ sukhasaññañca lahusaññañca okkamati pavisati phasseti sampāpuṇāti.
rows.english: 131.He arrives at blissful perception and light perception: he arrives at, enters, makes contact with, reaches, the perception of bliss and perception of lightness that are conascent with the consciousness whose object is the basic jhāna.

rows.pali: Sukhasaññā nāma upekkhāsampayuttasaññā.
rows.english: And it is perception associated with equanimity that is called “perception of bliss”;

rows.pali: Upekkhā hi santaṃ sukhanti vuttā.
rows.english: for equanimity is called “bliss” since it is peaceful.

rows.pali: Sāyeva ca saññā nīvaraṇehi ceva vitakkādīhi paccanīkehi ca vimuttattā lahusaññātipi veditabbā.
rows.english: And that same perception should be understood to be called “perception of lightness” too because it is liberated from hindrances and from the things that oppose it beginning with applied thought.

rows.pali: Taṃ okkantassa panassa karajakāyopi tūlapicu viya sallahuko hoti.
rows.english: But when he arrives at that state, his physical body too becomes as light as a tuft of cotton.

rows.pali: So evaṃ vāyukkhittatūlapicunā viya sallahukena dissamānena kāyena brahmalokaṃ gacchati.
rows.english: He goes to the Brahmā-world thus with a visible body as light as a tuft of cotton wafted by the wind.

rows.pali: Evaṃ gacchanto ca sace icchati pathavīkasiṇavasena ākāse maggaṃ nimminitvā padasā gacchati.
rows.english: 132.As he goes thus, if he wishes, he creates a path in space by means of the earth kasiṇa and goes on foot.

rows.pali: Sace icchati vāyokasiṇavasena vāyuṃ adhiṭṭhahitvā tūlapicu viya vāyunā gacchati.
rows.english: If he wishes, he resolves by means of the air kasiṇa that there shall be air, and he goes by air like a tuft of cotton.

rows.pali: Apica gantukāmatā eva ettha pamāṇaṃ.
rows.english: Moreover, the desire to go is the measure here.

rows.pali: "Sati hi gantukāmatāya" evaṃ katacittādhiṭṭhāno adhiṭṭhānavegukkhittova so issāsakhittasaro viya dissamāno gacchati.
rows.english: When there is the desire to go, one who has made his mental resolve in this way goes visibly, carried by the force of the resolution like an arrow shot by an archer.

rows.pali: Cittavasena kāyaṃ pariṇāmetīti kāyaṃ gahetvā citte āropeti.
rows.english: 133.He converts his body to accord with his mind: he takes the body and mounts it on the mind.

rows.pali: Cittānugatikaṃ karoti sīghagamanaṃ.
rows.english: He makes its going swift to coincide with that of the mind;

rows.pali: Cittagamanaṃ hi sīghaṃ hoti.
rows.english: for the mind’s mode of going is swift.

rows.pali: Sukhasaññañca lahusaññañca okkamatīti rūpakāyārammaṇena iddhicittena sahajātaṃ sukhasaññañca lahusaññañca okkamatīti.
rows.english: He arrives at blissful perception and light perception: he arrives at perception of bliss and perception of lightness that are conascent with the supernormal- power consciousness whose object is the material body.

rows.pali: Sesaṃ vuttanayeneva veditabbaṃ.
rows.english: The rest should be understood in the way already described.

rows.pali: Idaṃ pana cittagamanameva hoti.
rows.english: But here there is only the going of consciousness.21

rows.pali: Evaṃ adissamānena kāyena gacchanto panāyaṃ kiṃ tassa adhiṭṭhānacittassa uppādakkhaṇe gacchati, udāhu ṭhitikkhaṇe bhaṅgakkhaṇe vāti vutte tīsupi khaṇesu gacchatīti thero āha.
rows.english: 134.When it was asked, “As he goes with an invisible body thus, does he go at the moment of the resolution-consciousness’s arising or at the moment of its presence or at the moment of its dissolution?”, an elder replied, “He goes in all three moments.”

rows.pali: Kiṃ pana so sayaṃ gacchati nimmitaṃ pesetīti.
rows.english: —“But does he go himself, or does he send a creation?”

rows.pali: Yathāruci karoti.
rows.english: —“He does as he pleases.

rows.pali: Idha panassa sayaṃ gamanameva āgataṃ.
rows.english: But here it is only the going himself that has been given [in the text].”

rows.pali: Manomayanti adhiṭṭhānamanena nimmitattā manomayaṃ.
rows.english: 135.Mind-made: mind-made because created by the mind in resolution.

rows.pali: Ahīnindriyanti idaṃ cakkhusotādīnaṃ saṇṭhānavasena vuttaṃ.
rows.english: Lacking no faculty: this refers to the shape of the eye, ear, etc.;

rows.pali: Nimmitarūpe pana pasādo nāma natthi.
rows.english: but there is no sensitivity in a created visible form.22

rows.pali: Sace iddhimā caṅkamati nimmitopi tattha caṅkamatītiādi sabbaṃ sāvakanimmitaṃ sandhāya vuttaṃ.
rows.english: If the possessor of supernormal power walks up and down, the creation walks up and down there too, etc., all refers to what a disciple creates;

rows.pali: Buddhanimmito pana yaṃ yaṃ bhagavā karoti, taṃ tampi karoti.
rows.english: but what the Blessed One creates does whatever the Blessed One does,

rows.pali: Bhagavato rucivasena aññampi karotīti.
rows.english: and it also does other things according to the Blessed One’s pleasure.

rows.pali: Ettha ca yaṃ so iddhimā idheva ṭhito dibbena cakkhunā rūpaṃ passati, dibbāya sotadhātuyā saddaṃ suṇāti, cetopariyañāṇena cittaṃ pajānāti, na ettāvatā kāyena vasaṃ vatteti.
rows.english: 136.When this possessor of supernormal power, while remaining here sees a visible object with the divine eye, hears a sound with the divine ear element, knows consciousness with the penetration of minds, he does not wield bodily power in doing that.

rows.pali: Yampi so idheva ṭhito tena brahmunā saddhiṃ santiṭṭhati sallapati sākacchaṃ samāpajjati, ettāvatāpi na kāyena vasaṃ vatteti.
rows.english: And when, while remaining here, he stands with that Brahmā, converses, enters into communication with that Brahmā, he does not wield bodily power in doing that.

rows.pali: Yampissa dūrepi santike adhiṭṭhātītiādikaṃ adhiṭṭhānaṃ, ettāvatāpi na kāyena vasaṃ vatteti.
rows.english: And when he makes his resolve described in the way beginning “though far, he resolves upon nearness,” he does not wield bodily power in doing that.

rows.pali: Yampi so dissamānena vā adissamānena vā kāyena brahmalokaṃ gacchati, ettāvatāpi na kāyena vasaṃ vatteti.
rows.english: And when he goes to the Brahmā-world with a visible or an invisible body, he does not wield bodily power in doing that.

rows.pali: Yañca kho so tassa brahmuno purato rūpaṃ abhinimminātītiādinā nayena vuttavidhānaṃ āpajjati, ettāvatā kāyena vasaṃ vatteti nāmaṃ.
rows.english: But when he enters upon the process described in the way beginning, “He creates a visible form before that Brahmā, mind-made,” then he wields bodily power in doing that.

rows.pali: Sesaṃ panettha kāyena vasaṃ vattanāya pubbabhāgadassanatthaṃ vuttanti ayaṃ tāva adhiṭṭhānā iddhi.
rows.english: The rest, however, is said here for the purpose of showing the stage prior to the wielding of the bodily power. This, firstly, is (i) success by resolve (§45).

rows.pali: 398.Vikubbanāya pana manomayāya ca idaṃ nānākaraṇaṃ.
rows.english: 137.The difference between (i) success as transformation and (ii) success as the mind-made [body], is as follows (see §22, 24, 25, 45).

rows.pali: Vikubbanaṃ tāva karontena "so pakativaṇṇaṃ vijahitvā kumārakavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, nāgavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, supaṇṇavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, asuravaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, indavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, devavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, brahmavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, samuddavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, pabbatavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, sīhavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, byagghavaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, dīpivaṇṇaṃ vā dasseti, hatthimpi dasseti, assampi dasseti, rathampi dasseti, pattimpi dasseti, vividhampi senābyūhaṃ dassetī"ti (paṭi. ma. 3.13) evaṃ vuttesu kumārakavaṇṇādīsu yaṃ yaṃ ākaṅkhati, taṃ taṃ adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ.
rows.english: (i) One, firstly, who performs a transformation [406] should resolve upon whatever he chooses from among the things beginning with the appearance of a boy, described as follows: “He abandons his normal appearance and shows the appearance of a boy or the appearance of a nāga (serpent), or the appearance of a supaṇṇa (winged demon), or the appearance of an asura (demon), or the appearance of the Ruler [of Gods] (Indra), or the appearance of some [other sensual-sphere] deity, or the appearance of a Brahmā, or the appearance of the sea, or the appearance of a rock, or the appearance of a lion, or the appearance of a tiger, or the appearance of a leopard, or he shows an elephant, or he shows a horse, or he shows a chariot, or he shows a foot soldier, or he shows a manifold military array” (Paṭis II 210).

rows.pali: Adhiṭṭhahantena ca pathavīkasiṇādīsu aññatarārammaṇato abhiññāpādakajjhānato vuṭṭhāya attano kumārakavaṇṇo āvajjitabbo.
rows.english: 138.And when he resolves he should emerge from the fourth jhāna that is the basis for direct-knowledge and has one of the things beginning with the earth kasiṇa as its object, and he should advert to his own appearance as a boy.

rows.pali: Āvajjitvā parikammāvasāne puna samāpajjitvā vuṭṭhāya evarūpo nāma kumārako homīti adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ.
rows.english: After adverting and finishing the preliminary work, he should attain again and emerge, and he should resolve thus: “Let me be a boy of such and such a type.”

rows.pali: Saha adhiṭṭhānacittena kumārako hoti devadatto viya (cūḷava. 333).
rows.english: Simultaneously with the resolve consciousness he becomes the boy, just as Devadatta did (Vin I 185; Dhp-a I 139).

rows.pali: Esa nayo sabbattha.
rows.english: This is the method in all instances.

rows.pali: Hatthimpi dassetītiādi panettha bahiddhāpi hatthiādidassanavasena vuttaṃ.
rows.english: But he shows an elephant, etc., is said here with respect to showing an elephant, etc., externally.

rows.pali: Tattha hatthī homīti anadhiṭṭhahitvā hatthī hotūti adhiṭṭhātabbaṃ, assādīsupi eseva nayoti.
rows.english: Herein, instead of resolving, “Let me be an elephant,” he resolves, “Let there be an elephant.” The same method applies in the case of the horse and the rest.

rows.pali: Ayaṃ vikubbanā iddhi.
rows.english: This is success as transformation.

rows.pali: 399.Manomayaṃ kātukāmo pana pādakajjhānato vuṭṭhāya kāyaṃ tāva āvajjitvā vuttanayeneva susiro hotūti adhiṭṭhāti, susiro hoti.
rows.english: 139.(ii) One who wants to make the mind-made [body] should emerge from the basic jhāna and first advert to the body in the way already described, and then he should resolve, “Let it be hollow.” It becomes hollow.

rows.pali: Athassa abbhantare aññaṃ kāyaṃ āvajjitvā parikammaṃ katvā vuttanayeneva adhiṭṭhāti, tassa abbhantare añño kāyo hotūti.
rows.english: Then he adverts to another body inside it, and having done the preliminary work in the way already described, he resolves, “Let there be another body inside it.”

rows.pali: So taṃ muñjamhā īsikaṃ viya kosiyā asiṃ viya karaṇḍāya ahiṃ viya ca abbāhati.
rows.english: Then he draws it out like a reed from its sheath, like a sword from its scabbard, like a snake from its slough.

rows.pali: Tena vuttaṃ "idha bhikkhu imamhā kāyā aññaṃ kāyaṃ abhinimmināti rūpiṃ manomayaṃ sabbaṅgapaccaṅgiṃ ahīnindriyaṃ.
rows.english: Hence it is said: “Here a bhikkhu creates from this body another body possessing visible form, mind-made, with all its limbs, lacking no faculty.

rows.pali: Seyyathāpi puriso muñjamhā īsikaṃ pavāheyya, tassa evamassa ayaṃ muñjo ayaṃ īsikā, añño muñjo aññā īsikā, muñjamhātveva īsikā pavāḷhā"tiādi (paṭi. ma. 3.14).
rows.english: Just as though a man pulled out a reed from its sheath and thought thus: ‘This is the sheath; this is the reed; the sheath is one, the reed is another, it was from the sheath that the reed was pulled out’” (Paṭis II 210), and so on.

rows.pali: Ettha ca yathā īsikādayo muñjādīhi sadisā honti, evaṃ manomayarūpaṃ iddhimatāsadisameva hotīti dassanatthaṃ etā upamā vuttāti.
rows.english: And here, just as the reed, etc., are similar to the sheath, etc., so too the mind-made visible form is similar to the possessor of supernormal power, and this simile is given in order to show that.

rows.pali: Ayaṃ manomayā iddhi.
rows.english: This is success as the mind-made [body].

rows.pali: 365.Idāni yāsaṃ lokikābhiññānaṃ vasena ayaṃ samādhibhāvanā abhiññānisaṃsāti vuttā, tā abhiññā sampādetuṃ yasmā pathavīkasiṇādīsu adhigatacatutthajjhānena yoginā yogo kātabbo.
rows.english: 1. It was said above with reference to the mundane kinds of direct- knowledge that this development of concentration “provides … the benefit of the kinds of direct-knowledge” (XI.122). Now, in order to perfect those kinds of direct-knowledge the task must be undertaken by a meditator who has reached the fourth jhāna in the earth kasiṇa, and so on.

rows.pali: Evañhissa sā samādhibhāvanā adhigatānisaṃsā ceva bhavissati thiratarā ca, so adhigatānisaṃsāya thiratarāya samādhibhāvanāya samannāgato sukheneva paññābhāvanaṃ sampādessati.
rows.english: And in doing this, not only will this development of concentration have provided benefits in this way, it will also have become more advanced; and when he thus possesses concentration so developed as to have both provided benefits and become more advanced, he will then more easily perfect the development of understanding.

rows.pali: Tasmā abhiññākathaṃ tāva ārabhissāma.
rows.english: So meanwhile we shall deal with the explanation of the kinds of direct-knowledge now.

rows.pali: Bhagavatā hi adhigatacatutthajjhānasamādhīnaṃ kulaputtānaṃ samādhibhāvanānisaṃsadassanatthañceva uttaruttari paṇītapaṇītadhammadesanatthañca "so evaṃ samāhite citte parisuddhe pariyodāte anaṅgaṇe vigatūpakkilese mudubhūte kammaniye ṭhite āneñjappatte iddhividhāya cittaṃ abhinīharati abhininnāmeti.
rows.english: 2.In order to show the benefits of developing concentration to clansmen whose concentration has reached the fourth jhāna, and in order to teach progressively refined Dhamma, five kinds of mundane direct-knowledge have been described by the Blessed One. They are: (1) the kinds of supernormal power, described in the way beginning, “When his concentrated mind is thus purified, bright, unblemished, rid of defilement, and has become malleable, wieldy, steady, and attained to imperturbability,1 he directs, he inclines, his mind to the kinds of supernormal power.

rows.pali: So anekavihitaṃ iddhividhaṃ paccanubhoti ekopi hutvā bahudhā hotī"tiādinā (dī. ni. 1.238) nayena iddhividhaṃ, dibbasotadhātuñāṇaṃ, cetopariyañāṇaṃ, pubbenivāsānussatiñāṇaṃ, sattānaṃ cutūpapāte ñāṇanti pañca lokikābhiññā vuttā.
rows.english: He wields the various kinds of supernormal power. Having been one, he becomes many …” (D I 77); (2) the knowledge of the divine ear element; (3) the knowledge of penetration of minds; (4) the knowledge of recollection of past lives; and (5) the knowledge of the passing away and reappearance of beings.

rows.pali: Tattha ekopi hutvā bahudhā hotītiādikaṃ iddhivikubbanaṃ kātukāmena ādikammikena yoginā odātakasiṇapariyantesu aṭṭhasu kasiṇesu aṭṭha aṭṭha samāpattiyo nibbattetvā –
rows.english: If a meditator wants to begin performing the transformation by supernormal power described as, “Having been one, he becomes many,” etc., he must achieve the eight attainments in each of the eight kasiṇas ending with the white kasiṇa. He must also have complete control of his mind in the following fourteen ways: [374]

rows.pali: Kasiṇānulomato, kasiṇapaṭilomato, kasiṇānulomapaṭilomato, jhānānulomato, jhānapaṭilomato, jhānānulomapaṭilomato, jhānukkantikato, kasiṇukkantikato, jhānakasiṇukkantikato, aṅgasaṅkantito, ārammaṇasaṅkantito, aṅgārammaṇasaṅkantito, aṅgavavatthāpanato, ārammaṇavavatthāpanatoti.
rows.english: (i) in the order of the kasiṇa, (ii) in the reverse order of the kasiṇa, (iii) in the order and reverse order of the kasiṇa, (iv) in the order of the jhāna, (v) in the reverse order of the jhāna (vi) in the order and reverse order of the jhāna, (vii) skipping jhāna, (viii) skipping kasiṇa, (ix) skipping jhāna and kasiṇa, (x) transposition of factors, (xi) transposition of object, (xii) transposition of factors and object, (xiii) definition of factors, and (xiv) definition of object.

rows.pali: Imehi cuddasahi ākārehi cittaṃ paridametabbaṃ.
rows.english: 3. But what is “in the order of the kasiṇa” here?

rows.pali: 366.Katamaṃ panettha kasiṇānulomaṃ - pe - katamaṃ ārammaṇavavatthāpananti.
rows.english: What is “definition of object”?

rows.pali: Idha bhikkhu pathavīkasiṇe jhānaṃ samāpajjati, tato āpokasiṇeti evaṃ paṭipāṭiyā aṭṭhasu kasiṇesu satakkhattumpi sahassakkhattumpi samāpajjati, idaṃ kasiṇānulomaṃ nāma.
rows.english: (i) Here a bhikkhu attains jhāna in the earth kasiṇa, after that in the water kasiṇa, and so progressing through the eight kasiṇas, doing so even a hundred times, even a thousand times, in each one. This is called in the order of the kasiṇas.

rows.pali: Odātakasiṇato pana paṭṭhāya tatheva paṭilomakkamena samāpajjanaṃ kasiṇapaṭilomaṃ nāma.
